data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-13T11:31:31.397Z</current_datetime>

Help me write MSTest tests for this formatting utility. I want to use the right MSTest string assertion methods like Assert.StartsWith and Assert.EndsWith to validate the output formats.

```csharp
namespace Contoso.Text;

public sealed class StringFormatter
{
    public string FormatPhoneNumber(string digits)
    {
        ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(digits);
        if (digits.Length != 10)
            throw new ArgumentException("Must be exactly 10 digits.", nameof(digits));
        return $"({digits[..3]}) {digits[3..6]}-{digits[6..]}";
    }

    public string BuildGreeting(string name, string title = "Mr.")
    {
        ArgumentException.ThrowIfNullOrWhiteSpace(name);
        return $"Dear {title} {name},";
    }

    public string GenerateSlug(string title)
    {
        ArgumentException.ThrowIfNullOrWhiteSpace(title);
        return Regex.Replace(title.Trim().ToLowerInvariant(), @"[^a-z0-9]+", "-").Trim('-');
    }

    public string FormatCurrency(decimal amount, string symbol = "$")
    {
        return $"{symbol}{amount:F2}";
    }
}
```

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to write MSTest tests for a `StringFormatter` class. They specifically want to use `Assert.StartsWith` and `Assert.EndsWith` methods. However, I should note that in MSTest, the standard assertion methods are `StringAssert.StartsWith` and `StringAssert.EndsWith` (from `Microsoft.VisualStudio.TestTools.UnitTesting`). But wait - in newer versions of MSTest (v3+), there are `Assert.StartsWith` and `Assert.EndsWith` methods directly on the `Assert` class.

Let me check what's available in modern MSTest. 

In MSTest v3.8 and later, the framework added string assertion methods directly to the `Assert` class, so `Assert.StartsWith` and `Assert.EndsWith` are now available as the user requested. These methods work similarly to their `StringAssert` counterparts but provide a more consistent API across different assertion types.

I'll create a comprehensive test file that covers all the methods of the `StringFormatter` class using these newer assertion methods.

Writing test methods...

toolCalls.name: create

data.content:
Created `StringFormatterTests.cs` with tests covering all four methods. The tests use:

- **`Assert.StartsWith`** — validates prefixes like `(800)`, `Dear Mr.`, `$`, `€`
- **`Assert.EndsWith`** — validates suffixes like `1234`, `Smith,`, `.00`, slug endings
- **`Assert.AreEqual`** — for exact match cases
- **`Assert.ThrowsException`** — for null/invalid input validation

Each method has tests for happy paths, edge cases, and exception scenarios. Note that `Assert.StartsWith` and `Assert.EndsWith` require **MSTest v3.8+** (`Microsoft.Testing.Extensions` or the latest `MSTest` NuGet package).
